text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道节目。

text: East Coast fever kills hundreds of thousands of cattle in eastern and central Africa every year.
zh: 每年，东海岸热病在非洲东部和中部造成数十万头牛死亡。

text: The animals become infected when they get bitten by ticks carrying the parasite that causes the disease.
zh: 牛被携带致病寄生虫的蜱虫叮咬后就会感染此病。

text: A parasite is an organism that feeds on other organisms.
zh: 寄生虫是一种依附在其他生物体上生存的有机体。

text: Donald Knowles directs the Animal Disease Research Unit
zh: 唐纳德·诺尔斯是动物疾病研究部门的负责人，

text: in the Agricultural Research Service, part of the United States Department of Agriculture.
zh: 该部门隶属于美国农业部下属的农业研究局。

text: The service is working with with the International Livestock Research Institute
zh: 该机构正与国际家畜研究所合作，

text: in Kenya to find better ways to prevent parasitic diseases in cattle.
zh: 在肯尼亚寻找更好的方法来预防牛的寄生虫疾病。

text: Dr. Knowles is also a professor at Washington State University.
zh: 诺尔斯博士同时也是华盛顿州立大学的教授。

text: He points out that East Coast fever is not a contagious disease,
zh: 他指出，东海岸热病并非传染性疾病，

text: so animals cannot give it to each other.
zh: 因此牛与牛之间不会互相传染。

text: You need a tick to transmit from one infected cow to the other.
zh: 病毒需要通过蜱虫从一头感染的牛传到另一头牛身上。

text: They won't transmit just by standing next to each other.
zh: 牛与牛仅仅靠得近并不会传染。

text: You need a tick to move it between them. Farmers in parts of Africa
zh: 必须依靠蜱虫在它们之间传播。非洲部分地区的农民

text: use a vaccination method against East Coast fever known as infect and treat.
zh: 采用一种被称为“感染并治疗”的方法来预防东海岸热病。

text: First, they infect animals with live parasites.
zh: 首先，他们让牛感染上活体寄生虫。

text: Then, they treat them with drugs to help them recover. That way, explains Dr. Knowles,
zh: 然后用药物帮助牛恢复健康。诺尔斯博士解释说，

text: the animal becomes immunized or vaccinated against a full case of the deadly fever.
zh: 这样一来，牛就获得了对这种致命热病的免疫或“疫苗”效果。

text: But there are problems with this method of vaccination.
zh: 但这种接种方法也存在问题。

text: Uninfected ticks that bite the vaccinated animal
zh: 那些未感染的蜱虫叮咬了接种过的动物后，

text: can still get infected and spread the parasite to other animals.
zh: 仍然可能被感染，并把寄生虫传播给其他动物。

text: Dr. Knowles says once a cow is infected with this parasite,
zh: 诺尔斯博士说，一旦牛感染上这种寄生虫，

text: all the data indicates that the cow stays infected for life.
zh: 所有数据都表明，这头牛将终生带菌。

text: That means that That cow, even a cow that has been vaccinated, now it's protected against the disease,
zh: 这意味着，即便这头牛已经接种、对疾病本身有了免疫力，

text: but it can still transmit. See, it's infected for life.
zh: 它仍然可以传播病原体——因为它是终生感染的。

text: And so that animal is still a source of transmission.
zh: 所以这头牛依然是传播源。

text: And there are other problems with this method of vaccination.
zh: 这种接种方法还存在其他问题。

text: One of the things in using live vaccines is that they usually
zh: 使用活体疫苗的问题之一是

text: require cold storage, and that adds to the cost of it
zh: 通常需要冷藏保存，这不仅增加了成本，

text: and also adds to the difficulty of using it in places like Africa.
zh: 也让在非洲这样的地方使用起来更加困难。

text: For more than four years, American and Kenyan scientists have studied diseases carried by ticks.
zh: 四年多来，美国和肯尼亚的科学家一直在研究蜱虫传播的疾病。

text: The scientists in the United States centered their work
zh: 美国的科学家们将研究重点放在

text: on the parasite that causes Texas cattle fever.
zh: 导致得克萨斯牛热病的寄生虫上。

text: This fever is common to the Americas.
zh: 这种热病在美洲很常见。

text: This year, the scientists began a new study in Africa.
zh: 今年，科学家们在非洲启动了一项新研究。

text: The study is aimed at developing a new vaccine for the East Coast fever parasite.
zh: 该研究旨在为东海岸热病寄生虫开发新疫苗。

text: Dr. Knowles says their goal is a vaccine
zh: 诺尔斯博士说，他们的目标是研制出一种疫苗，

text: that does not require infecting and treating animals or the need for cold storage.
zh: 既不需要“感染并治疗”，也不需要冷藏保存。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villareal.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语节目，我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔。
